channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Falz clarifies he never supported any politician in 2023 election
item.description: Nigerian rapper Falz has clarified that he never openly supported any politician or political party during the 2023 election. In an interview on Arise TV’s Prime Time programme, Falz addressed public ...

item.title: Brampton MPP faces election finance probe over undeclared family newspaper ads and hotel expenses
item.description: Hardeep Grewal, the Progressive Conservative MPP for Brampton East, is under investigation for alleged violations of election finance rules. The Ontario NDP alleges he failed to declare campaign adver...

item.title: Housemaid and minor arrested for theft in Quezon City
item.description: A housemaid and a minor were arrested for theft in Quezon City, according to a recent news report. The incident involved the unauthorized taking of property from a private residence. Authorities confi...

item.title: New York's Rental Ripoff Report Sparks Concern Over Housing Sector Impact
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's Rental Ripoff Hearing Report, released last week, includes 23 policy recommendations aimed at regulating the private rental housing sector. While the report acknow...

item.title: Sessions Judge and Gunman Killed in Balochistan Attack
item.description: A sessions judge and his gunman were killed in a targeted attack in Balochistan's Mastung district, while an additional judge and another individual sustained critical injuries. The incident occurred ...

item.title: New $21m outreach program addresses rising homelessness on Mornington Peninsula
item.description: The article reports a significant increase in homelessness on the Mornington Peninsula, with numbers tripling in recent years. A new $21 million outreach team has been established to tackle the issue,...

item.title: Labor Conference Addresses Pauline Hanson's Influence and Political Tensions
item.description: The Australian Labor Party's 50th annual conference in Adelaide highlighted tensions with One Nation leader Pauline Hanson, though she was never directly named by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese. Alba...

item.title: Saboti MP Accuses UDA of Voter Importation in Trans Nzoia Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Saboti Member of Parliament Caleb Amisi has accused the ruling United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party of orchestrating a voter importation scheme in Trans Nzoia county, warning of serious consequences...

item.title: Hosepipe bans expose tensions over water shortages, ageing infrastructure and political division in England
item.description: The article examines how hosepipe bans introduced across large parts of England have become the focus of wider political and cultural disputes rather than simply a response to drought. Water companies...

item.title: Wildfire approaches B.C. farmhouse as doorbell video captures real-time danger
item.description: A doorbell camera captured footage of a wildfire rapidly advancing toward a farmhouse near Boston Bar, British Columbia, raising concerns for the safety of the couple residing there. The video, which ...

item.title: Supreme Court to Act if Metro Station Near Court Remains Closed: CJI
item.description: The Supreme Court has indicated it will take action if the metro station closest to its premises remains closed past noon. Chief Justice Surya Kant made this statement after the Supreme Court Bar Asso...

item.title: MCL adopts digital strategies to meet evolving reader demands
item.description: Mwananchi Communications Limited (MCL) is rapidly shifting from traditional print media to a digital-first model to adapt to changing reader behaviors. The company, which publishes Mwananchi, Mwanaspo...

item.title: Montreal woman loses limbs to sepsis after rare infection
item.description: A 43-year-old Montreal woman, Cammeilla Ramdass, lost both her hands and legs after a rare bacterial infection triggered septic shock. The incident began in January when she sought medical attention f...

item.title: Defense Secretary Teodoro Dismisses China's Accusations Over Ayungin Shoal Activities
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. rejected China's claims that Philippine construction activities on the BRP Sierra Madre violated a provisional agreement with Beijing, asserting that China has n...

item.title: Kentucky's Medicaid Reimbursement Cut May Lead to Higher Costs Despite Initial Savings
item.description: The article discusses the potential long-term consequences of a 4% cut in Kentucky’s Medicaid reimbursement rates. While the reduction may appear as a measure of fiscal discipline within the state bud...

item.title: NCC and BMA Collaborate to Combat Counterfeit Goods at South African Borders
item.description: The National Consumer Commission (NCC) and the Border Management Authority (BMA) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen efforts against counterfeit and unsafe goods entering Sou...

item.title: US Senator Accuses Nigerian Govt of Focusing on Lobbying Instead of Addressing Religious Violence
item.description: US Senator Ted Cruz has accused Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu administration of prioritizing lobbying efforts in Washington over addressing attacks on Christians and religious minorities. In a state...

item.title: Wildfires Ravage Southern Europe, Displace Thousands Amid Climate Crisis
item.description: Wildfires have been sweeping across Italy, France, and Spain, forcing thousands to evacuate their homes. The blazes, fueled by extreme heat and strong winds, have claimed three firefighter lives and c...

item.title: Impeachment Committee Delays Key Decisions as Ramaphosa's Testimony Becomes Central Issue
item.description: The South African Parliament's impeachment committee has delayed critical decisions on its proceedings, with President Cyril Ramaphosa's testimony emerging as a major point of contention. Political di...

item.title: Deputy PM Ishaq Dar Addresses Asean Forum on Regional Security and Kashmir Dispute
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar addressed the 33rd ARF Ministerial Meeting, emphasizing unresolved disputes like the Jammu and Kashmir conflict and India's unilateral actions as key threats to South A...
